inputs.label: HUMIDITY
inputs.implementation: Connect the data pin to any GPIO with a 4.7 kΩ pull-up resistor to VCC. Power at 3.3 V or 5 V. Wait at least 2 s between readings — the sensor is slow by design.

inputs.label: TEMPERATURE
inputs.implementation: DS18B20 uses 1-Wire — connect its data line to a GPIO with a 4.7 kΩ pull-up to VCC. For an NTC thermistor, form a voltage divider with a 10 kΩ fixed resistor and read the midpoint with an ADC pin.

inputs.label: LIGHT
inputs.implementation: Wire an LDR in a voltage divider with a 10 kΩ resistor and read the midpoint on an ADC pin. The BH1750 is a better choice for calibrated lux: use I²C at address 0x23.

inputs.label: MOVEMENT
inputs.implementation: The HC-SR501 outputs a digital HIGH on its OUT pin when motion is detected. Power it at 5 V and connect OUT to any GPIO. Adjust sensitivity and hold-time with the two on-board trimpots.

inputs.label: SOUND LEVEL
inputs.implementation: The MAX4466 outputs an analog voltage proportional to sound amplitude — connect its OUT to an ADC pin. Adjust gain with the on-board trimpot. Keep power traces clean and away from digital lines to reduce noise.

inputs.label: PRESSURE
inputs.implementation: The BMP280 uses I²C at address 0x76 or 0x77 — add 4.7 kΩ pull-ups on SDA/SCL. For a force-sensitive resistor, wire it as a voltage divider with a 10 kΩ resistor and read on an ADC pin.

inputs.label: PROXIMITY
inputs.implementation: HC-SR04: pull TRIG high for 10 µs, then measure how long ECHO stays high. Distance (cm) = duration_µs × 0.034 / 2. The VL53L0X is simpler — it uses I²C and handles all the timing internally.

inputs.label: MAGNETIC FIELD
inputs.implementation: The A3144 digital Hall sensor outputs LOW when a magnet is nearby and HIGH otherwise. Connect VCC to 5 V, GND to ground, and OUT to a GPIO with the internal pull-up enabled in firmware.

outputs.label: LED ARRAY
outputs.implementation: Put a 300–500 Ω resistor in series on the data line close to the MCU. Add a 100–470 µF capacitor across VCC and GND near the first pixel. Power from 5 V; add a level-shifter if your MCU runs at 3.3 V.

outputs.label: BUZZER
outputs.implementation: Drive a passive buzzer with PWM through a 100 Ω series resistor — vary the frequency to change pitch. An active buzzer only needs a digital HIGH/LOW signal; it generates its own fixed tone internally.

outputs.label: BINARY DISPLAY
outputs.implementation: Connect 8 LEDs to 8 GPIO output pins, each through a 220–470 Ω current-limiting resistor. Represent the sensor reading in binary: bit 7 on the leftmost LED, bit 0 on the rightmost.

outputs.label: MORSE CODE
outputs.implementation: Blink a single GPIO output. Convention: dot = 100 ms ON, dash = 300 ms ON, gap between symbols = 100 ms OFF, gap between letters = 300 ms OFF. Encode your sensor value as a number in Morse.

outputs.label: LCD SCREEN
outputs.implementation: The I²C LCD backpack typically sits at address 0x27 — connect SDA/SCL with 4.7 kΩ pull-ups. Call lcd.print() to display values. Twist the trimpot on the back to adjust contrast after soldering.

outputs.label: SERVO MOTOR
outputs.implementation: Generate a 50 Hz PWM signal. Pulse width ~1 ms = 0°, ~1.5 ms = 90°, ~2 ms = 180°. Power the servo from VCC (5 V), not from the MCU's regulated rail — servos draw high current on movement.

outputs.label: RGB MATRIX
outputs.implementation: The MAX7219 uses SPI (DIN, CS, CLK). On startup, configure the scan-limit register (0x0B) and intensity register (0x0A), then clear the display. Each row register (0x01–0x08) holds one byte of pixel data.

outputs.label: 7-SEGMENT
outputs.implementation: The TM1637 uses a 2-wire interface (CLK and DIO) — not standard I²C. Use a TM1637 library to display integers or floats directly. The module handles multiplexing internally; no extra resistors needed.

connectors.label: KEYCHAIN
connectors.implementation: Add two 3.2 mm drill holes (MountingHole_3.2mm_M3 footprint in KiCad) near one edge of the board, at least 3 mm from any copper trace. Loop a split ring or small carabiner through one hole after fabrication.

connectors.label: WEIRD SHAPE
connectors.implementation: Draw your PCB outline on the Edge.Cuts layer in KiCad. Keep all copper, vias, and pads at least 0.5 mm inside the edge. Avoid sharp inside corners — add a ≥ 0.5 mm fillet radius or the router bit will leave a chamfer anyway.

connectors.label: BIG BUTTON
connectors.implementation: Wire one side of the button to a GPIO pin and the other side to GND. Enable the MCU's internal pull-up so the pin reads HIGH when open and LOW when pressed. Add a 20 ms software debounce to ignore contact bounce.

connectors.label: USB-C POWER
connectors.implementation: Connect VBUS to your power rail through a 500 mA polyfuse, GND to ground. Add 5.1 kΩ resistors from each CC pin (CC1, CC2) to GND — these identify the device as a power sink and enable up to 900 mA from a compliant charger.

connectors.label: BADGE CLIP
connectors.implementation: Place two 2.7 mm drill holes 25 mm apart along the top edge to match a standard badge clip. Keep a 5 mm keepout zone around the holes clear of copper. Alternatively, add a 4.5 mm slot for a lanyard loop.

connectors.label: FRIDGE MAGNET
connectors.implementation: Design the PCB at standard 1.6 mm thickness. Leave the bottom side clear of tall components near the center. Glue 2–4 neodymium disc magnets (6×2 mm) symmetrically to the back with epoxy or foam tape so the board hangs level.

connectors.label: ROTARY KNOB
connectors.implementation: Connect the A and B pins to two GPIOs with 10 kΩ pull-ups (or enable internal pull-ups in firmware). Read the quadrature transitions to determine rotation direction and count. The EC11 also has a push-switch on SW/GND for click input.

connectors.label: CARD SWIPE
connectors.implementation: A 3-track mag-stripe reader outputs a CARD_PRESENT signal plus serial data pins (one per track). Monitor CARD_PRESENT on a GPIO and read the data pin during the active window. Parse the raw bitstream per ISO 7811 for the encoded data.
